chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: So a couple years ago, a woman I know who I'll call Maya

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: went through a lot of big changes in a short amount of time.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: She got married, she and her husband moved for his job

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: to a new city where she didn't know anyone.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: She started a new role working from home,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: all while managing her dad's new diagnosis of dementia.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: And to manage the stress of all this change,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: Maya doubled down on her physical and mental health.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: And she was able to get through the first two years

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: She exercised almost every day, she ate healthy foods,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: she went to therapy once a week.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: And these actions really helped.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: Her body got stronger, her mind got more resilient,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: but only up to a point.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: She was still struggling,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: often losing sleep in the middle of the night,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: feeling unfocused, unmotivated during the day.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: Maya was doing everything that doctors typically tell us to do

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: to be physically and mentally healthy,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: and yet something that she was able to do

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: and yet something was missing.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: What if I told you that what was missing for Maya

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: is also missing for billions of people around the world,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: and that it might be missing for you?

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: What if I told you that not having it

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: undermines our other efforts to be healthy

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: and can even shorten your lifespan?

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: I've been studying this for over a decade,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: and I've discovered that the traditional way we think about health

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: is incomplete.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: By thinking of our health as primarily physical and mental,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: we overlook what I believe is the greatest challenge

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: and the greatest opportunity of our time.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: Social health.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: While physical health is about our bodies

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: and mental health is about our minds,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: social health is about our relationships.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: And if you haven't heard this term before,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: that's because it hasn't yet made its way into mainstream vocabulary,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: yet it is equally important.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: Maya didn't yet have a sense of community in her new home.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: She wasn't seeing her family or her friends or her coworkers in person anymore,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: and she often went weeks only spending quality time with her husband.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: Her story shows us that we can't be fully healthy.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: We can't thrive if we take care of our bodies and our minds,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: but not our relationships.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: Similar to Maya,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: hundreds of millions of people around the world

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: go weeks at a time without talking to a single friend or family member.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: Globally, one in four people feel lonely,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: and 20 percent of adults worldwide

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: don't feel like they have anyone they can reach out to for support.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: Think about that.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: One in five people you encounter may feel like they have no one.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: This is more than heartbreaking,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: it's also a public health crisis.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: Disconnection triggers stress in the body,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: it weakens people's immune systems,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: it puts them at a risk, greater risk,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: of stroke, heart disease, diabetes, dementia, depression and early death.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: Social health is essential for longevity.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: So you might be wondering,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: what does it look like to be socially healthy?

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: What does that even mean?

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: Well, it's about developing close relationships

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: with your family, your friends, your partner, yourself.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: It's about having regular interaction with your coworkers, your neighbors.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: It's about feeling like you belong to a community.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: Being socially healthy is about having the right quantity

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: and quality of connection for you.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: And Maya's story is one example of how social health challenges come up.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: In my work, I hear many others.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: Stories like Jay, a freshman in college,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: who's eager to get involved in campus,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: yet is having a hard time fitting in with people in his dorm

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: and often feels homesick.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: Or Serena and Ali, a couple juggling the chaos of young kids

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: with demanding jobs.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: They rarely have time to see friends or spend time one-on-one.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: Or Henry, recently retired,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: who cherishes time with his spouse

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: and yet feels untethered without his team anymore

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: and wishes he could see his kids and grandkids more often.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: These stories show that social health is relevant to each of us

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: at every life stage.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: So if you're not sure where to start,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: try the 531 guideline from my book.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: It goes like this.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: Aim to interact with five different people each week

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: to strengthen at least three close relationships overall

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: and to spend one hour a day connecting.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: Let's dig into these.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: So first, interact with five different people each week.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: Just like eating a variety of vegetables and other food groups

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: is more nutritious,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: research has shown that interacting with a variety of people

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: is more rewarding.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: So your five could include close loved ones,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: casual acquaintances, even complete strangers.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: In fact, in one study that I love,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: people who just smiled, made eye contact and chit-chatted with a barista

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: felt happier and a greater sense of belonging

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: than people who just rushed to get their coffee and go.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: Next, strengthen at least three close relationships.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: Okay, we've all heard of a to-do list,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: but I would like to invite you to write a to-love list.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: Who matters most to you?

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: Who can you be yourself with?

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: Make sure that you invest in the names of at least three of the people

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: that you write down,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: by scheduling regular time together,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: by showing a genuine interest in their lives,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: and also by opening up about the experiences that you're going through.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: And I'm often asked, does it have to be in person?

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: Does texting count?

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: Studies have shown that face-to-face is ideal,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: so do that whenever possible,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: but there are absolutely still benefits to staying connected virtually.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: And last, spend one hour a day on meaningful connection.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: Okay, if you're an introvert,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: right now you're probably thinking,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: one hour sounds like a lot.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: I get it.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: It might be surprising, but I'm actually also an introvert.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: However, keep in mind that just like getting eight hours of sleep at night,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: the exact amount that's right for you personally

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: might be higher or lower.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: But if you're thinking that one hour a day sounds like way too much

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: because you're just way too busy,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: I challenge you.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: Adults in the US spend an average of four and a half hours each day

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: on their smartphones.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: So instead of scrolling on social media,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: text a friend.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: Instead of reading news headlines,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: write a thank-you card.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: Instead of listening to a podcast,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: call a family member.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: Maya put this into practice

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: by scheduling recurring hangouts with a new local friend that she made,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: by attending community events and dropping cards off

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: in her neighbor's mailboxes,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: by planning trips to see family

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: and inviting friends in other cities to come visit.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: And bolstering her social health

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: made more of a difference

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: than focusing solely on her physical and mental health ever could.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: And I know this,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: because Maya is actually me.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: I am so passionate about sharing tools to be socially healthy,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: because honestly, I need them too.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: And the 531 guideline is one way

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: that we can be proactive and intentional about our relationships.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: And that is really the point,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: be proactive and intentional about your social health.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: So zooming out beyond the steps that you and I take individually,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: together we need to shape a society that thrives through social health.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: Over the next decade,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: I envision educators championing social health in schools.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: And just like kids build their physical muscles in gym class,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: they'll exercise their social muscles in connection class.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: Over the next decade,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: I see our cities and neighborhoods being designed with social health in mind,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: where vibrant gathering places foster unity,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: and community builders are empowered to bring them to life.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: Over the next decade,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: I believe that social health will become as ingrained

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: in our collective consciousness as mental health is today.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: Because not that long ago,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: mental health was a taboo topic shrouded in stigma,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: and now public figures talk openly about it.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: There's an entire industry to support it,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: and more and more people think of going to therapy like going to the gym.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: In this future, loneliness will subside,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: just like smoking subsided when we recognized and treated it

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: as a public health issue.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: In this future,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: I hope that social health will become so deeply woven

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: into the fabric of our culture

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: that no one needs the 531 guideline anymore.

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: So to get there,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: make relationships your priority,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: not only for you but also for the people you love,

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: because the beauty of nurturing your own social health

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: is that it naturally enriches the social health

chunks.title: Why_Social_Health_Is_Key_to_Happiness_and_Longevity
chunks.text: Transcriptions byändern polítick

text: So a couple years ago, a woman I know who I'll call Maya went through a lot of big changes in a short amount of time. She got married, she and her husband moved for his job to a new city where she didn't know anyone. She started a new role working from home, all while managing her dad's new diagnosis of dementia. And to manage the stress of all this change, Maya doubled down on her physical and mental health. And she was able to get through the first two years She exercised almost every day, she ate healthy foods, she went to therapy once a week. And these actions really helped. Her body got stronger, her mind got more resilient, but only up to a point. She was still struggling, often losing sleep in the middle of the night, feeling unfocused, unmotivated during the day. Maya was doing everything that doctors typically tell us to do to be physically and mentally healthy, and yet something that she was able to do and yet something was missing. What if I told you that what was missing for Maya is also missing for billions of people around the world, and that it might be missing for you? What if I told you that not having it undermines our other efforts to be healthy and can even shorten your lifespan? I've been studying this for over a decade, and I've discovered that the traditional way we think about health is incomplete. By thinking of our health as primarily physical and mental, we overlook what I believe is the greatest challenge and the greatest opportunity of our time. Social health. While physical health is about our bodies and mental health is about our minds, social health is about our relationships. And if you haven't heard this term before, that's because it hasn't yet made its way into mainstream vocabulary, yet it is equally important. Maya didn't yet have a sense of community in her new home. She wasn't seeing her family or her friends or her coworkers in person anymore, and she often went weeks only spending quality time with her husband. Her story shows us that we can't be fully healthy. We can't thrive if we take care of our bodies and our minds, but not our relationships. Similar to Maya, hundreds of millions of people around the world go weeks at a time without talking to a single friend or family member. Globally, one in four people feel lonely, and 20 percent of adults worldwide don't feel like they have anyone they can reach out to for support. Think about that. One in five people you encounter may feel like they have no one. This is more than heartbreaking, it's also a public health crisis. Disconnection triggers stress in the body, it weakens people's immune systems, it puts them at a risk, greater risk, of stroke, heart disease, diabetes, dementia, depression and early death. Social health is essential for longevity. So you might be wondering, what does it look like to be socially healthy? What does that even mean? Well, it's about developing close relationships with your family, your friends, your partner, yourself. It's about having regular interaction with your coworkers, your neighbors. It's about feeling like you belong to a community. Being socially healthy is about having the right quantity and quality of connection for you. And Maya's story is one example of how social health challenges come up. In my work, I hear many others. Stories like Jay, a freshman in college, who's eager to get involved in campus, yet is having a hard time fitting in with people in his dorm and often feels homesick. Or Serena and Ali, a couple juggling the chaos of young kids with demanding jobs. They rarely have time to see friends or spend time one-on-one. Or Henry, recently retired, who cherishes time with his spouse and yet feels untethered without his team anymore and wishes he could see his kids and grandkids more often. These stories show that social health is relevant to each of us at every life stage. So if you're not sure where to start, try the 531 guideline from my book. It goes like this. Aim to interact with five different people each week to strengthen at least three close relationships overall and to spend one hour a day connecting. Let's dig into these. So first, interact with five different people each week. Just like eating a variety of vegetables and other food groups is more nutritious, research has shown that interacting with a variety of people is more rewarding. So your five could include close loved ones, casual acquaintances, even complete strangers. In fact, in one study that I love, people who just smiled, made eye contact and chit-chatted with a barista felt happier and a greater sense of belonging than people who just rushed to get their coffee and go. Next, strengthen at least three close relationships. Okay, we've all heard of a to-do list, but I would like to invite you to write a to-love list. Who matters most to you? Who can you be yourself with? Make sure that you invest in the names of at least three of the people that you write down, by scheduling regular time together, by showing a genuine interest in their lives, and also by opening up about the experiences that you're going through. And I'm often asked, does it have to be in person? Does texting count? Studies have shown that face-to-face is ideal, so do that whenever possible, but there are absolutely still benefits to staying connected virtually. And last, spend one hour a day on meaningful connection. Okay, if you're an introvert, right now you're probably thinking, one hour sounds like a lot. I get it. It might be surprising, but I'm actually also an introvert. However, keep in mind that just like getting eight hours of sleep at night, the exact amount that's right for you personally might be higher or lower. But if you're thinking that one hour a day sounds like way too much because you're just way too busy, I challenge you. Adults in the US spend an average of four and a half hours each day on their smartphones. So instead of scrolling on social media, text a friend. Instead of reading news headlines, write a thank-you card. Instead of listening to a podcast, call a family member. Maya put this into practice by scheduling recurring hangouts with a new local friend that she made, by attending community events and dropping cards off in her neighbor's mailboxes, by planning trips to see family and inviting friends in other cities to come visit. And bolstering her social health made more of a difference than focusing solely on her physical and mental health ever could. And I know this, because Maya is actually me. I am so passionate about sharing tools to be socially healthy, because honestly, I need them too. And the 531 guideline is one way that we can be proactive and intentional about our relationships. And that is really the point, be proactive and intentional about your social health. So zooming out beyond the steps that you and I take individually, together we need to shape a society that thrives through social health. Over the next decade, I envision educators championing social health in schools. And just like kids build their physical muscles in gym class, they'll exercise their social muscles in connection class. Over the next decade, I see our cities and neighborhoods being designed with social health in mind, where vibrant gathering places foster unity, and community builders are empowered to bring them to life. Over the next decade, I believe that social health will become as ingrained in our collective consciousness as mental health is today. Because not that long ago, mental health was a taboo topic shrouded in stigma, and now public figures talk openly about it. There's an entire industry to support it, and more and more people think of going to therapy like going to the gym. In this future, loneliness will subside, just like smoking subsided when we recognized and treated it as a public health issue. In this future, I hope that social health will become so deeply woven into the fabric of our culture that no one needs the 531 guideline anymore. So to get there, make relationships your priority, not only for you but also for the people you love, because the beauty of nurturing your own social health is that it naturally enriches the social health of everyone you connect with. Thank you. Transcriptions byändern polítick
